The policies for the common good
developed under Theodore Roose-
welt and bearing his name.


The reorganization Of the federal
house and senate so that both shall
be responsive to public opinion and
no longer dominated by privileged
interests.


Strong and effective control of
railroads and the just regulation of
their rates.


A protective tariff, measured by
the difference in the cost of produc-
ton here and abroad


Federal conservation of our nat
ural resources.


An issue Of bonds for such
amount AS will enable the reclama-
ton service to complete without de-
lay the government irrigation prof
ects now in process Of construction


A liberal and practical policy OF
internal waterway improvement,
and. II necessary, the issue of bonds
for that work.
A limitation Of the power of in
junction so as not to imperil the
1 liberty of any man without notice
g Legislation which will end child
and sweatshops of the land.


The proposed amendment of the
power to tax incomes.





If you favor these things, vote
election.
